Politics.headline: Peeni Henare asks MPs to rebuild trust in Parliament
Politics.detail: Peeni Henare left Parliament this week with a final address full of stories and humour, asking MPs to rebuild trust.

Politics.headline: Trump demands 'team effort' to defend Strait of Hormuz as he urges allies to send warships
Politics.detail: Amidst the ongoing conflict with Iran, President Trump is calling for a global coalition to ensure the Strait of Hormuz remains open for shipping, requesting allied nations to contribute warships.

Politics.headline: Reform UK government would replace top civil servants with those 'more likely to implement party's priorities'
Politics.detail: Reform UK is proposing to replace high-ranking civil servants to ensure the party's agenda is executed effectively if they gain power.

Politics.headline: Brazil pulls visa of Trump adviser who asked to visit Bolsonaro in prison
Politics.detail: Brazilian President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva has revoked the visa of Darren Beattie, a Trump advisor, who requested to visit Bolsonaro in prison.

Politics.headline: Trump urges world to help open Strait of Hormuz; U.S. Embassy in Baghdad hit
Politics.detail: As the Iran war continues, and oil prices surge, President Trump is calling for international assistance to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad was hit.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks lose ground as war with Iran keeps pressure on oil prices
Finance & Economy.detail: Wall Street's losses deepened as the ongoing fallout from the war in Iran keeps pushing oil prices higher, ratcheting up inflationary pressure on the global economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: 'The sums don't add up': UK farmers struggle as Iran war drives up costs
Finance & Economy.detail: UK farmers are facing increased costs due to the Iran war, impacting their ability to operate profitably.

Finance & Economy.headline: European shares log second week of losses as Mideast war fuels inflation fears
Finance & Economy.detail: European shares experienced their second consecutive week of losses as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East contributes to concerns about rising inflation.

Finance & Economy.headline: UAE suspends limited operations at Fujairah after drone attack; What does this mean for the global economy?
Finance & Economy.detail: Following a drone attack, the UAE has suspended some operations at Fujairah, raising concerns about the potential impact on the global economy.

Finance & Economy.headline: Stocks slide again – Markets closed the week on a soft note amid further signs of an escalation in the conflict in Iran.
Finance & Economy.detail: The S&P 500 index erased a near 1% gain earlier in the day to close 0.6% lower, continuing a run of steady declines over recent sessions, due to escalation of conflict in Iran.

Technology.headline: New Website 'Channel Surfer' Reinvents the Cable TV Couch Vegetation Experience for 2026
Technology.detail: A new website called 'Channel Surfer' aims to recreate the traditional cable TV experience for modern audiences.

Technology.headline: Rivian's Highly Anticipated R2 Starts Sales in Spring With 330 Miles of Range
Technology.detail: Rivian's new R2 electric vehicle is set to launch this spring, offering an estimated range of 330 miles.

Technology.headline: AI Burning Man happens next week –what to expect at Nvidia GTC 2026
Technology.detail: Nvidia's GTC 2026, featuring an 'AI Burning Man,' is scheduled for next week, promising new advancements and showcases in AI technology.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Warns of Hackers Supercharging Cyberattacks With AI
Technology.detail: Microsoft is cautioning that cybercriminals are increasingly leveraging AI to enhance their attacks, automating phishing, malware creation, and reconnaissance efforts.

Technology.headline: AI Glasses Shift Into Momentum Mode, Shipments Grow 322% in 2025
Technology.detail: Shipments of AI glasses experienced substantial growth in 2025, increasing by 322% as the technology gains traction.

Science.headline: Hair may grow in a completely different way than scientists once believed
Science.detail: Instead of being pushed out from the root, new research shows that moving cells inside the follicle actually pull the hair.

Science.headline: Scientists may have finally solved the mystery of strange plume-like structures hidden deep inside the Greenland ice sheet
Science.detail: New research suggests they form through thermal convection—slow, swirling motions driven by differences in temperature and density.

Science.headline: Cambridge scientists have discovered a light-powered chemical reaction that lets researchers modify complex drug molecules at the final stages of development
Science.detail: Unlike traditional methods, this new technique relies on photocatalysis to selectively alter specific bonds in a molecule.

Science.headline: Spiders and insects may not be fan favorites, but they are vital to the health of ecosystems—and scientists barely know how they're doing
Science.detail: Researchers found that nearly 90% of North America's insect and arachnid species have no conservation status, highlighting a significant gap in our understanding and protection of these critical creatures.

Science.headline: Researchers have created “Smart Underwear,” a wearable device that measures flatulence by detecting hydrogen produced by gut microbes
Science.detail: Early tests suggest people may pass gas about 32 times a day, and this technology could help monitor gut health.

Health.headline: App-based therapy helps men improve control over premature ejaculation
Health.detail: A new app-based therapy has been shown to help men improve their control over premature ejaculation, offering a convenient and accessible treatment option.

Health.headline: Selfish chromosomes hijack Overdrive gene to eliminate rival sperm
Health.detail: Researchers have discovered how selfish chromosomes hijack the Overdrive gene to eliminate rival sperm, providing insights into the genetic conflicts that can occur during reproduction.

Health.headline: Study highlights benefits of specialist resource centers for autistic pupils
Health.detail: A recent study has highlighted the benefits of specialist resource centers for autistic pupils, demonstrating their positive impact on academic and social outcomes.

Health.headline: Study reveals how disordered proteins function without stable structure
Health.detail: A new study has revealed how disordered proteins can function without a stable structure, challenging traditional understanding of protein function and opening new avenues for research.

Health.headline: Vitamin B2 metabolism helps cancer cells resist ferroptosis
Health.detail: Research indicates that vitamin B2 metabolism plays a role in helping cancer cells resist ferroptosis, a form of cell death, suggesting potential therapeutic targets.

Sports.headline: Sumit Antil Secures Gold at World Para Athletics Grand Prix
Sports.detail: Sumit Antil won a gold medal in the javelin throw at the World Para Athletics Grand Prix in New Delhi.

Sports.headline: Deadly shooting delays fan entry at Players Championship
Sports.detail: A fatal shooting near TPC Sawgrass, which left two people dead, caused the tournament organizers to delay fan entry at the Players Championship. The suspect fled onto the golf course before being arrested.

Sports.headline: Women's Tennis Beats Georgia Southern
Sports.detail: The Coastal Carolina women's tennis team defeated Georgia Southern with a score of 4-2, improving their season record to 7-2. Aida Mitrache secured the match-winning point on court four.

Sports.headline: Teenager Max Dowman makes history to seal vital Arsenal win over Everton
Sports.detail: Max Dowman, at 16 years old, became Arsenal's youngest scorer in a 2-0 victory against Everton, making him the Premier League's youngest goal scorer. His goal secured a win that moved Arsenal ten points clear in the title race.

Sports.headline: Inter held to 1-1 draw by Atalanta as title race remains open
Sports.detail: Inter Milan's Serie A match against Atalanta ended in a 1-1 draw, with Nikola Krstovic scoring the equalizer for Atalanta. Inter now holds a lead of eight points over AC Milan in the title race.

Entertainment.headline: Badshah gets death threat from Lawrence Bishnoi gang
Entertainment.detail: Singer-rapper Badshah received death threats from Lawrence Bishnoi gang members, who warned him against tarnishing Haryana's culture after backlash over his song 'Tateeree'.

Entertainment.headline: Akshay Kumar reacts to being clicked with Twinkle Khanna, kids after Jaya Bachchan's comment on paps
Entertainment.detail: Akshay Kumar addressed concerns about paparazzi invading privacy, mentioning that it would be worrisome if nobody recognized or photographed him, while also acknowledging Jaya Bachchan's perspective on media behavior.

Entertainment.headline: Ryan and Rory deliver one of the most iconic C2C side stage performances ever!
Entertainment.detail: Ryan & Rory's UK debut at the C2C festival was a huge success, drawing comparisons to Ashley McBryde's iconic 2018 performance and leaving the audience impressed with their energetic and engaging show.

Entertainment.headline: Meet one of the last true paparazzi
Entertainment.detail: Pascal Rostain, a traditional paparazzi photographer, discussed how the industry has changed due to smartphones and social media.

Entertainment.headline: Dubai's current population is more than double compared to almost twenty years ago
Entertainment.detail: Dubai's population has more than doubled in the last 20 years to 3.7 million, impacting the property market.

Crime & Justice.headline: FBI: Synagogue attacker died by suicide
Crime & Justice.detail: The suspect in the Michigan synagogue attack died from a self-inflicted gunshot wound, the FBI said at a news conference on Friday.

Crime & Justice.headline: Officials give update on synagogue attack
Crime & Justice.detail: Officials in Michigan gave an update about Thursday's synagogue car ramming attack that the FBI is investigating as a 'targeted act of violence against the Jewish community'.

Crime & Justice.headline: New details from Virginia, Michigan attacks
Crime & Justice.detail: The U.S. is on high alert after two separate domestic attacks on Thursday: A shooting at Old Dominion University, and a car ramming at a Michigan synagogue.

Crime & Justice.headline: Latin American drug kingpin wanted by U.S. is arrested in Bolivia.
Crime & Justice.detail: Notorious Latin American narco trafficker Sebastian Marset, who eluded police for years, was handed over to U.S. authorities after his arrest Friday in Bolivia.

Crime & Justice.headline: Virginia university assailant dead, three injured in shooting: Police.
Crime & Justice.detail: A shooting at Old Dominion University has left the assailant dead and three others injured, according to police.

Education.headline: Teachers, personnel to receive P7,000 clothing allowance
Education.detail: The clothing and uniform allowance for fiscal year 2026 would be released not earlier than the first working day of April, and DepEd will strictly implement the wearing of the newly prescribed national uniform starting school year 2026-2027 this June. The Second Congressional Commission on Education (EDCOM 2) has launched the LGU Resource Center, a one-stop resource designed to help local government units implement education reforms and address the country's learning crisis.

Education.headline: Course correction: Indians pause plans to study overseas
Education.detail: Parents are making more calculated decisions before sending their children abroad due to tighter visa policies, tougher job markets, and currency fluctuations. Several global universities are establishing campuses in India, making it a more attractive education destination.

Education.headline: Rendeavour and Wellington College Education to open global British School in Tatu City, Kenya
Education.detail: Rendeavour and Wellington College Education will open a co-educational day and boarding school in Tatu City, Kenya, in September 2028, which will accommodate 1,500 students aged 3-18. Wellington College International Kenya aims to provide an outstanding British education, attracting students from across Africa and the world.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines Today (March 15): Top National, Business News, Sports News, Education News & World News with Weather Updates & Thought of the Day
Education.detail: Several universities across India are expanding digital learning programmes and hybrid classrooms. Education authorities are encouraging schools to introduce climate awareness and sustainability lessons, while new scholarship initiatives aim to support students pursuing STEM.

Education.headline: Education News Today
Education.detail: The Hindu reports on education-related news, including declining job prospects for B.Com graduates, the need for engineering education to cater to India's needs in space, defence, and drones, and how art and education are transforming slums into classrooms. Additionally, the outlet discusses medical students turning to YouTube and coaching apps due to missing professors.

Lifestyle.headline: 8 things retirees who feel most connected to younger generations do differently than the ones who feel increasingly invisible to them
Lifestyle.detail: Some retirees stay connected by acknowledging the unique challenges faced by younger generations, staying current without trying to be young, and creating new shared experiences. They also embrace technology to connect with loved ones.

Lifestyle.headline: Delhi wakes up to thunder, lightning and rain; IMD forecasts more storms across North India
Lifestyle.detail: Delhi experienced thunder, lightning, and light rain on Sunday, March 15, 2026, a welcome change from the hot weather, with the India Meteorological Department forecasting more thunderstorms, gusty winds, and rainfall across northwest India in the coming days. The weather pattern is attributed to a western disturbance affecting the region.

Lifestyle.headline: OIL PRICE WATCH as of March 15, 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Fuel prices are expected to surge in the coming week, with diesel potentially rising by P19.30 to P22.30 per liter and gasoline by P14 to P17 per liter. The price adjustments are attributed to dwindling supply due to intensifying attacks by the United States and Israel against Iran, which have paralyzed maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

Lifestyle.headline: Weekly Tarot Card Readings: Tarot prediction for March 15-21, 2026
Lifestyle.detail: A horoscope provides tarot predictions for March 15-21, 2026.

Lifestyle.headline: Rene Redzepi steps down from Noma and fine dining's 'brigade' culture comes under scrutiny
Lifestyle.detail: The downfall of the world's top chef has raised questions about whether time is up for the storied bullying and abuse of fine dining kitchens.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Gavin Newsom: Trump is worsening America's drug crisis. Here's what California is doing to stop him
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An op-ed from California's governor: The Trump administration has abandoned American families just as the country was making headway in our fight against the overdose epidemic.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Why are cars still killing so many people in San Francisco?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: OPINION: Speed cameras and a new mayoral safety initiative still haven't slowed the pace of pedestrian deaths; there have been five already in San Francisco in 2026, Allison Arieff writes.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial Board. Gavin Newsom's taxpayer-funded PR blitz.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The editorial board discusses Gavin Newsom's taxpayer-funded PR blitz.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial Board. South Dakota joins the lab-grown meat panic.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Washington Post's editorial board comments on South Dakota joining the lab-grown meat panic.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on the Iran war and international law: it's worse than a mistake; it's a crime.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Guardian's editorial board discusses the Iran war and international law, calling it worse than a mistake.

World.headline: Trump Urges World Powers to Send Warships to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: Following increased tensions and military actions between the U.S. and Iran, including attacks on oil infrastructure, U.S. President Donald Trump has called on countries that rely on oil from the Strait of Hormuz to send warships to help keep the passage open and safe. He stated the U.S. would assist and that 'many countries' would be participating in this effort.

World.headline: US-Israeli Strike Kills 15 at Isfahan Factory, Iranian Media Says
World.detail: A missile strike on an industrial area in Isfahan, Iran, reportedly killed at least 15 people. According to Iran's Fars news agency, the attack targeted a factory producing heating and cooling equipment.

World.headline: Ruling Communist Party's Office Vandalized in Cuba During Riot Over Blackouts
World.detail: A rally protesting power cuts and food shortages in Moron, Cuba, turned violent, resulting in an attack on a local Communist Party office. Videos circulating on social media showed a fire and individuals throwing rocks at the building while shouting 'liberty'.

World.headline: Xinhua World News Summary: U.S. Claims Strike on Kharg Island
World.detail: The U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) claimed that U.S. forces executed a large-scale precision strike on Kharg Island, a key Iranian oil export hub, destroying naval mine storage facilities, missile storage bunkers, and other military sites while preserving the oil infrastructure. Iran's Foreign Minister warned that any attack on Iran's energy infrastructure would result in retaliation against regional facilities linked to U.S. companies.

World.headline: Elections for New Leadership in Progress in Vietnam
World.detail: Vietnam has begun its election of deputies to the 16th National Assembly and People's Councils for the 2026–2031 term, with nearly 79 million eligible voters expected to participate. Prime Minister Phạm Minh Chinh is also seeking to implement a 100-year vision to transform Hanoi into a prosperous and modern city.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim Treasury to pay local suppliers in ZiG under new price guidelines
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwean government will now pay local suppliers in ZiG (Zimbabwe Gold) currency under new price guidelines, aiming to stabilize the economy and promote the use of the local currency.

Zimbabwe.headline: Stop treating road projects as temporary cosmetic exercises
Zimbabwe.detail: An article in NewsDay Zimbabwe criticizes the government's approach to road construction, urging them to move beyond superficial fixes and invest in long-term, sustainable infrastructure development.

Zimbabwe.headline: Maid jailed 36 months for masterminding US$80 000 heist
Zimbabwe.detail: A domestic worker was sentenced to 3 years in prison for her involvement in the theft of US$80,000 from her employer.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Vigil Diary 14th March 2026
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Vigil is a protest held outside the Zimbabwe Embassy in London, highlighting human rights issues and calling for political change in Zimbabwe; the diary entry likely details the events and concerns raised during the latest vigil.

Zimbabwe.headline: Man sets stepmother on fire
Zimbabwe.detail: A 22-year-old man has been arrested after allegedly setting his stepmother on fire.
